title: CLAUDE.md
text: arcopt (Adaptive Regularization using Cubics R) optimization package implementing cubic regularization methods (ARC) local optimization problems common statistics applied research. Primary Users: R users (statisticians, applied researchers) Problem Scale: Small medium (typically 2-500 parameters) GitHub: https://github.com/marcus-waldman/arcopt

title: CLAUDE.md
text: optimizer implements hierarchical 4-layer system: Orchestration Layer: Main loop (Algorithm 1) controls iteration flow dispatches sub-algorithms Core Solvers Layer: Newton step computation (Algorithm 1a) eigendecomposition cubic solver (Algorithm 5a) Adaptation Layer: Convergence checks (Algorithm 0) σ (sigma) regularization updates (Algorithms 2a/2b) Safeguards Layer: Indefiniteness handling (Algorithms 6a/6b), box constraint truncation (Algorithm 7), linear equality constraint handling (Algorithm 8)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Hessian-Centric: Accurate second derivative information prioritized. Hessian sources order preference: analytic > automatic differentiation > finite differences > quasi-Newton approximation. QN-ARC mode available via use_qn = TRUE. Robust Default: Single entry point arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess) sensible defaults; users need manually tune regularization select solvers Saddle Point Escape: Cubic regularization naturally handles negative curvature without explicit eigenvector computation Constraint Handling: Box constraints via step truncation; linear equality constraints via null-space transformation

code: library(arcopt)  rosenbrock <- function(x) {   (1 - x[1])^2 + 100 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)^2 }  rosenbrock_gr <- function(x) {   c(-2 * (1 - x[1]) - 400 * x[1] * (x[2] - x[1]^2),     200 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)) }  rosenbrock_hess <- function(x) {   matrix(c(1200 * x[1]^2 - 400 * x[2] + 2, -400 * x[1],            -400 * x[1],                     200),          2, 2) }  result <- arcopt(   x0 = c(-1.2, 1),   fn = rosenbrock,   gr = rosenbrock_gr,   hess = rosenbrock_hess )

title: Solver modes: cubic, trust-region fallback, and quasi-Newton polish
text: cubic subproblem eigendecomposes true Hessian writes step eigenbasis. single negative eigenvalue enough produce step negative-curvature direction, saddle escape true Hessian automatic. small example, consider two-component Gaussian mixture likelihood started symmetric near-saddle point: Started symmetric point near origin, BFGS-class methods land saddle report convergence; arcopt’s cubic mode steps negative-curvature direction recovers class means.
code: set.seed(42) y <- c(rnorm(100, -2, 1), rnorm(100, 2, 1))  mixture_nll <- function(theta) {   -sum(log(0.5 * dnorm(y, theta[1], 1) + 0.5 * dnorm(y, theta[2], 1))) } mixture_gr <- function(theta) {   p1 <- 0.5 * dnorm(y, theta[1], 1)   p2 <- 0.5 * dnorm(y, theta[2], 1)   w1 <- p1 / (p1 + p2)   w2 <- p2 / (p1 + p2)   -c(sum(w1 * (y - theta[1])), sum(w2 * (y - theta[2]))) } mixture_hess <- function(theta, h = 1e-5) {   d <- 2L   h_mat <- matrix(0, d, d)   for (i in seq_len(d)) {     e_i <- numeric(d)     e_i[i] <- h     h_mat[, i] <- (mixture_gr(theta + e_i) - mixture_gr(theta - e_i)) /       (2 * h)   }   0.5 * (h_mat + t(h_mat)) }  res <- arcopt(c(0.01, 0.01), mixture_nll, mixture_gr, mixture_hess,               control = list(trace = 0)) res$par               # near (-2, 2) modulo label permutation #> [1]  1.866497 -1.980722 res$diagnostics$solver_mode_final #> [1] "cubic" min(eigen(res$hessian, only.values = TRUE)$values)  # > 0: local min #> [1] 73.04552

title: Solver modes: cubic, trust-region fallback, and quasi-Newton polish
text: Cubic regularization can stall flat-ridge regimes – iterations regularization parameter sigma_k driven floor, step-acceptance ratio rho_k near 1, yet gradient bounded away zero Hessian positive definite near-singular weakly identified subspace. arcopt detects sliding-window check four signals , hold ten consecutive iterations, swaps cubic subproblem trust-region subproblem. transition one-way default-. well-conditioned problems detector simply fire, run terminates cubic mode: fallback’s value shows problems weakly identified parameter subspaces – e.g., Dirichlet-process-style mixtures unused stick-breaking segments, cubic-configuration exhausts iteration budget strongly indefinite saddle trust-region fallback fires reaches true local minimum. regime data- dependency-heavy inline example; see JSS manuscript referenced package README worked case. want disable fallback explicitly:
code: rosen_fn <- function(x) (1 - x[1])^2 + 100 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)^2 rosen_gr <- function(x) {   c(-2 * (1 - x[1]) - 400 * x[1] * (x[2] - x[1]^2),     200 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)) } rosen_hess <- function(x) {   matrix(c(1200 * x[1]^2 - 400 * x[2] + 2, -400 * x[1],            -400 * x[1],                     200), 2, 2) }  res <- arcopt(c(-1.2, 1), rosen_fn, rosen_gr, rosen_hess,               control = list(trace = 0)) res$diagnostics$solver_mode_final #> [1] "cubic" res$diagnostics$ridge_switches #> [1] 0 arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess,        control = list(tr_fallback_enabled = FALSE))

title: Solver modes: cubic, trust-region fallback, and quasi-Newton polish
text: strongly convex healthy basin cubic regularization parameter dropped floor Hessian essentially constant across iterates, every hess() call recomputes second derivatives contribute negligibly step quality. Polish mode recognizes regime via five-signal detector replaces cubic subproblem Wolfe line search along BFGS-approximated Newton direction, suspending hess() calls either convergence revert. Polish opt-(qn_polish_enabled = FALSE default). Enable long-running smooth problems expensive Hessians (analytic AD via Stan, finite differences, pipeline second derivative takes appreciable wall-clock time): five-dimensional smooth-convex problem polish detector fires initial cubic-Newton approach steps, remaining iterations consume zero hess() calls – typically saving 30-50% Hessian budget loss accuracy.
code: fn   <- function(x) sum(0.5 * x^2 + x^4 / 12) gr   <- function(x) x + x^3 / 3 hess <- function(x) diag(1 + x^2) x0   <- rep(10, 5)  res_default <- arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess,                       control = list(maxit = 50, trace = 0)) res_polish  <- arcopt(x0, fn, gr, hess,                       control = list(maxit = 50, trace = 0,                                      qn_polish_enabled = TRUE))  c(default_hess = res_default$evaluations$hess,   polish_hess  = res_polish$evaluations$hess) #> default_hess  polish_hess  #>            9            6  res_polish$diagnostics$solver_mode_final     # "qn_polish" #> [1] "qn_polish" res_polish$diagnostics$qn_polish_switches    # 1 #> [1] 1

title: Solver modes: cubic, trust-region fallback, and quasi-Newton polish
text: defaults designed users need touch mode switches. cubic mode handles indefiniteness saddle escape; trust-region fallback handles flat-ridge stagnation; polish reserved problems hess() genuinely expensive basin large enough detector window fill. practical heuristic: iterate enters saddle stalls – right default. Make sure hess() returns true Hessian, positive-definite proxy. arcopt exhausts iteration budget problem locally well-conditioned – inspect result$diagnostics$ridge_switches. 0 gradient bounded away zero, may flat-ridge regime; TR-fallback already default. Hessian-supplied run fast clock time per-call Hessian expensive (Stan AD, FD), enable qn_polish_enabled = TRUE check result$diagnostics$qn_polish_switches. cases diagnostic fields tell mode actually carried run, per-mode controls ?arcopt_advanced_controls provide tuning surface.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: ARC algorithm iteratively minimizes cubic regularization model: $$m_k(s) = f_k + g_k^\top s + \frac{1}{2} s^\top H_k s +   \frac{\sigma_k}{3} \|s\|^3$$ \(\sigma_k\) adapted observed model accuracy. arcopt may transparently fall back trust-region subproblem flat-ridge regimes (optionally, opt-) line-search BFGS polish quadratic attraction basin. transitions observable via result$diagnostics; algorithmic details tunable thresholds documented \link{arcopt_advanced_controls}.

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
text: arcopt Hessian-centric: analytic hess function strongly recommended. analytic form unavailable, set control$use_qn = TRUE obtain Hessian-free quasi-Newton updates (see advanced-controls page).

title: Adaptive Regularization using Cubics Optimizer — arcopt
code: # \donttest{ # Rosenbrock function rosenbrock <- function(x) (1 - x[1])^2 + 100 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)^2  rosenbrock_gr <- function(x) {   c(-2 * (1 - x[1]) - 400 * x[1] * (x[2] - x[1]^2),     200 * (x[2] - x[1]^2)) }  rosenbrock_hess <- function(x) {   matrix(c(     1200 * x[1]^2 - 400 * x[2] + 2, -400 * x[1],     -400 * x[1], 200   ), 2, 2) }  result <- arcopt(   x0 = c(-1.2, 1),   fn = rosenbrock,   gr = rosenbrock_gr,   hess = rosenbrock_hess )  print(result$par)      # Should be near c(1, 1) #> [1] 0.9999994 0.9999989 print(result$value)    # Should be near 0 #> [1] 3.138905e-13 # }

what: Trust-region fallback (cubic to TR on flat-ridge detection)
title: Advanced Control Parameters for arcopt — arcopt_advanced_controls
text: Cubic regularization can stagnate "flat-ridge" regimes – iterations regularization floor pinned, model predictions matching objective (\(\rho \approx 1\)), gradient stalling, Hessian positive-definite nearly singular. outside local-error-bound condition Yue, Zhou & (2018) cubic regularization guaranteed converge quadratically degenerate minimizers. arcopt detects regime switches cubic subproblem trust-region subproblem. tr_fallback_enabled One-way cubic TR switch (default TRUE). tr_fallback_window Sliding-window length detector (default 10). tr_fallback_tol_ridge lambda_min(H) threshold defining "near-singular PD" Hessian (default 1e-3). tr_fallback_rho_tol Tolerance |rho - 1| "near-perfect model" signal (default 0.1). tr_fallback_grad_decrease_max Ratio latest oldest ||g||_inf gradient counts stagnant (default 0.9). tr_fallback_g_inf_floor Absolute lower bound ||g||_inf switch fire – keeps hybrid triggering true local minima (default 1e-6). tr_r0 Initial trust-region radius switch (default 1.0). tr_rmax Maximum trust-region radius (default 100). tr_eta1 TR step-acceptance threshold (default 0.25). tr_eta2 TR expansion threshold (default 0.75). tr_gamma_shrink Radius shrink factor poor step (default 0.25). tr_gamma_grow Radius grow factor -good boundary step (default 2.0).

title: Advanced Control Parameters for arcopt — arcopt_advanced_controls
text: iterate enters quadratic attraction basin strict local minimum, cubic regularization penalty decayed floor contributes negligible damping, arcopt still evaluates hess() every iteration. expensive Hessians (analytic AD via Stan, finite differences) dominates wall-clock time. Polish mode replaces cubic subproblem Wolfe line search along BFGS-approximated Newton direction, skipping hess() calls convergence BFGS approximation drifts. default v0.2.0 existing manuscript benchmark problems converge five-signal healthy-basin detector accumulates full window. Enable opt-long-running smooth problems expensive Hessians. qn_polish_enabled Enable cubic qn_polish bidirectional switch (default FALSE). qn_polish_window Sliding-window length healthy- basin detector (default 5). qn_polish_rho Minimum rho_k required throughout window (default 0.9). qn_polish_lambda_min Minimum lambda_min(H_k) required throughout window (default 1e-3). qn_polish_g_decay Maximum ratio consecutive ||g||_inf values; e.g. 0.5 requires 2x-per-iteration contraction (default 0.5). qn_polish_g_inf_floor Absolute lower bound ||g||_inf window start; prevents firing convergence (default 1e-8). qn_polish_c1, qn_polish_c2 Wolfe line-search constants (defaults 1e-4 0.9). qn_polish_alpha_max Initial step length tried line search (default 1.0). qn_polish_max_ls_iter Maximum line-search evaluations per iteration (default 20). qn_polish_max_fail Consecutive line-search failures trigger revert cubic mode (default 3). qn_polish_reenter_delay Cubic iterations required revert qn_polish may re-fire (default 5). qn_polish_curv_eps Curvature threshold skipping BFGS updates preserve PD (default 1e-10).

title: Advanced Control Parameters for arcopt — arcopt_advanced_controls

"hybrid" uses state-aware routing SR1-first BFGS-first orderings based current B's eigenstructure recent rho values. bfgs_tol Curvature tolerance BFGS update (default 1e-10). sr1_skip_tol SR1 skip-test tolerance (default 1e-8). sr1_restart_threshold Consecutive SR1 skips restart (default 5). qn_route_demote_rho rho counts "bad" step routing FSM (default 0.25). qn_route_promote_rho rho counts "good" step (default 0.5). qn_route_demote_k Consecutive bad steps "pd" routing mode demote back "indefinite" (default 2). qn_route_promote_k Consecutive good PD steps "indefinite" mode promote "pd" (default 3). qn_fd_refresh_k Consecutive bad-rho iterations "indefinite" mode trigger FD-Hessian refresh B (default 3). qn_stuck_refresh_k Iterations stuck "indefinite" mode without promotion force refresh (default 100). use_accel_qn EXPERIMENTAL. title: Flat-Ridge Detector for Cubic-to-Trust-Region Fallback — flat_ridge
text: detector maintains sliding window runtime diagnostics fires four following signals hold throughout window: Sigma pinned floor: sigma_k <= 10 * sigma_min every iteration window. Near-perfect model: |rho_k - 1| < rho_tol every iteration window (model reduction matches actual reduction). Gradient stagnant: ratio recent ||g||_inf oldest window exceeds grad_decrease_max (less 10 percent decrease window default). Hessian ill-conditioned: smallest Hessian eigenvalue recent iteration satisfies lambda_min < tol_ridge. includes classical "flat-ridge" regime (small positive lambda_min) "stuck--indefinite-saddle" regime (negative lambda_min), cubic regularization loses grip . absolute floor g_inf_floor also enforced: trigger fire ||g||_inf already typical convergence tolerance, preventing spurious triggers true local minima. detector empirical proxy local-error-bound (EB) violation (Yue, Zhou, 2018). EB, cubic regularization attains Q-quadratic convergence even degenerate minima; detector fires precisely regime EB practically vacuous theoretical convergence guarantee applies.

what: Healthy-Basin Detector for Cubic-to-QN-Polish Transition — healthy_basin
title: Healthy-Basin Detector for Cubic-to-QN-Polish Transition — healthy_basin

title: Healthy-Basin Detector for Cubic-to-QN-Polish Transition — healthy_basin
text: detector maintains sliding window runtime diagnostics fires five following signals hold throughout window: Newton step accepted: step_type == "newton" every iteration window. Indicates cubic subproblem invoked (Cholesky succeeded Newton step passed ratio test). High ratio: rho_k >= rho_polish (default 0.9) throughout window. quadratic model accurately predicting actual reduction. Hessian well-conditioned PD: lambda_min(H_k) >=     lambda_min_polish (default 1e-3) throughout window. just "Cholesky succeeded" strictly bounded away zero. Superlinear gradient decay: ||g_k||_inf /     ||g_{k-1}||_inf <= g_decay_polish (default 0.5) every consecutive pair window. strongest signal quadratic attraction basin. convergence floor: ||g_0||_inf >     g_inf_floor_polish window start (default 1e-8). Prevents firing convergence Newton-accept gradient-decay trivially satisfied. Contrast flat-ridge detector (flat_ridge): detector fires stagnation (gradient decreasing); one fires healthy convergence (gradient decreasing super- linearly). expected mutually exclusive practice.

title: Solve Trust-Region Subproblem via Eigendecomposition (Algorithm 5c) — solve_tr_eigen
text: trust-region counterpart solve_cubic_eigen. structural difference constraint: cubic solver enforces lambda = sigma * ||s||, trust-region solver enforces ||s|| <= radius. reduce (H + lambda*) s = -g lambda >= max(0, -lambda_min(H)). Three cases handled: Interior: H positive definite unconstrained Newton step -H^{-1} g lies inside trust region. Returned lambda = 0 on_boundary = FALSE. Easy boundary: unique lambda > max(0, -lambda_min) places ||s(lambda)|| = radius. Found Newton secular equation. Hard case: Hessian indefinite gradient nearly orthogonal smallest-eigenvalue eigenvector. Resolved per -Sorensen setting lambda = -lambda_min(H) adding component along smallest eigenvector reach boundary. Recommended n <= 500 due O(n^3) eigendecomposition cost.

title: Wolfe Line Search — wolfe_line_search
text: Strong Wolfe conditions: Armijo (sufficient decrease): \(f(x + \alpha d) \le f(x) + c_1 \alpha g_x^\top d\) Curvature: \(|g(x + \alpha d)^\top d| \le c_2 |g_x^\top d|\) algorithm first brackets interval containing Wolfe point, bisects within bracket locate one. Bisection used simplicity; quadratic cubic interpolation Nocedal & Wright Sec. 3.5 can added future refinement. Returns success = FALSE strong Wolfe alpha found within max_iter evaluations. caller can still consult alpha (associated trial point) decide whether take best-known step reject iteration.

what: QN-polish fallback for the strongly-convex basin
title: arcopt 0.1.1 (development)
text: arcopt() now supports opt-bidirectional cubic <-> qn_polish transition (control$qn_polish_enabled, default FALSE) replaces cubic subproblem Wolfe line search along BFGS-approximated Newton direction iterate entered quadratic attraction basin strict local minimum. switch, current exact Hessian HkH_k warmstarts BFGS approximation B0B_0; subsequent iterations call hess() unless revert. Motivation: near strict minimum σ\sigma decayed floor, cubic penalty numerically inactive yet arcopt keeps evaluating hess(). expensive Hessians (AD via Stan, FD) per-iteration cost dominates polish phase. QN-polish skips evaluations preserves Newton’s local quadratic convergence via exact-H warm start plus BFGS’s superlinear updates. Healthy-basin detector fires five signals hold sliding window (default width 5): Newton step accepted every iteration, ρk≥0.9\rho_k \ge 0.9, λmin(Hk)≥10−3\lambda_{\min}(H_k) \ge 10^{-3} (strict well-conditioned PD), superlinear gradient decay (∥gk∥∞/∥gk−1∥∞≤0.5\|g_k\|_\infty / \|g_{k-1}\|_\infty \le 0.5), ∥g0∥∞\|g_0\|_\infty convergence floor (10−810^{-8}). Bidirectional: BFGS drifts non-PD (Cholesky fails), search direction descent, Wolfe line search fails qn_polish_max_fail consecutive iterations (default 3), solver reverts cubic mode, recomputes H(xk)H(x_k), enters cooldown period (qn_polish_reenter_delay, default 5 cubic iterations) qn_polish may re-fire. Prevents ping-ponging letting detector catch basin signal cubic re-stabilizes. New control parameters: qn_polish_enabled, qn_polish_window, qn_polish_rho, qn_polish_lambda_min, qn_polish_g_decay, qn_polish_g_inf_floor, qn_polish_c1, qn_polish_c2, qn_polish_alpha_max, qn_polish_max_ls_iter, qn_polish_max_fail, qn_polish_reenter_delay, qn_polish_curv_eps. New return fields: qn_polish_switches (count cubic->qn_polish transitions), qn_polish_reverts (count reversions), hess_evals_at_polish_switch (Hessian-eval count first switch; difference final evaluations$hess quantifies savings). solver_mode_final now includes "qn_polish" possible value. New internal functions: wolfe_line_search() (Nocedal & Wright 2006, Algorithms 3.5 + 3.6), init_healthy_basin_state(), update_healthy_basin_state(), check_healthy_basin_trigger(). Empirical: smooth non-quadratic convex problem (f(x)=∑(0.5xi2+xi4/12)f(x) = \sum (0.5 x_i^2 + x_i^4/12), n=5n=5, x0=10⋅𝟏x_0 = 10 \cdot \mathbf{1}) polish mode default thresholds reduces Hessian evaluations 9 6 (33%); loose thresholds (W=3W=3, ρpolish=0.3\rho_{\text{polish}}=0.3) 9 4 (56%). Rosenbrock detector fire default settings banana-valley iterations produce intermittent ρ\rho dips; Rosenbrock converges via cubic-path.

title: arcopt 0.1.1 (development)
text: arcopt() arcopt_qn() now include one-way cubic--trust-region fallback activates cubic-regularization subproblem stalls near-singular positive-definite regime (“flat ridge”). switch fires four runtime signals hold sliding window: () σk\sigma_k pinned floor, (ii) |ρk−1||\rho_k - 1| small (model accurate), (iii) ∥gk∥∞\|g_k\|_\infty stagnant across window, (iv) smallest Hessian eigenvalue strictly positive tr_fallback_tol_ridge. detector empirical proxy violation local-error-bound (EB) condition cubic regularization guaranteed converge quadratically (Yue, Zhou & 2018); four fire, convergence theory pure cubic regularization teeth, trust-region step-norm constraint principled alternative. New control parameters (tunable, sensible defaults): tr_fallback_enabled (default TRUE) — one-way cubic->TR switch. tr_fallback_window (default 10), tr_fallback_tol_ridge (default 1e-3), tr_fallback_rho_tol (default 0.1), tr_fallback_grad_decrease_max (default 0.9), tr_fallback_g_inf_floor (default 1e-6) — detector parameters. tr_rmax (default 1e6), tr_eta1 (default 0.25), tr_eta2 (default 0.75), tr_gamma_shrink (default 0.25), tr_gamma_grow (default 2.0) — trust-region update parameters. New return fields: solver_mode_final ("cubic" "tr"), ridge_switches (count cubic->TR transitions, 0 1 v1), radius_final (final trust-region radius solver switched TR mode). New internal functions: solve_tr_eigen() (trust-region subproblem via eigendecomposition, mirrors solve_cubic_eigen()), init_flat_ridge_state(), update_flat_ridge_state(), check_flat_ridge_trigger() (detector state machine). solve_tr_eigen() uses stats::uniroot ϕ(λ)=∥s(λ)∥−r\phi(\lambda) = \|s(\lambda)\| - r easy-case secular equation rather plain Newton. Plain Newton numerically unstable ill-conditioned indefinite Hessians (overshoots lower-bound neighborhood ∥s(λ)∥\|s(\lambda)\| blows ); uniroot unconditionally stable given valid bracket. Signal 4 flat-ridge detector broadened 0<λmin(H)<𝚝𝚘𝚕_𝚛𝚒𝚍𝚐𝚎0 < \lambda_{\min}(H) < \texttt{tol\_ridge} λmin(H)<𝚝𝚘𝚕_𝚛𝚒𝚍𝚐𝚎\lambda_{\min}(H) < \texttt{tol\_ridge} — detector now fires classical flat-ridge (small positive λmin\lambda_{\min}) stuck--indefinite-saddle (negative λmin\lambda_{\min}) regimes, since cubic regularization loses grip . σ↔r\sigma \leftrightarrow r duality cubic regularization trust-region step bounds formally established Dussault (2018; ARCq) Martínez & Raydan (2017); release’s adaptive switch two subproblem formulations appears novel. See todo/tr-fallback-hybrid-briefing.md design literature context.

title: arcopt 0.1.1 (development)
text: arcopt_qn() now seeds initial Hessian approximation B0B_0 one-time finite-difference Hessian computed supplied gradient hess function provided (previously seeded identity matrix). Costs 2n2n gradient evaluations startup dramatically improves saddle-escape behavior. recover classical identity-init BFGS convention, pass hess = function(x) diag(length(x)). qn_method = "hybrid" now uses state-aware routing SR1-first BFGS-first update priorities. mode switches based () smallest eigenvalue current BkB_k (Cholesky inertia) cubic-subproblem ratio ρk\rho_k recent steps. New control parameters: qn_route_demote_rho (default 0.25): ρ\rho counts bad step. qn_route_promote_rho (default 0.5): ρ\rho counts good step. qn_route_demote_k (default 2): consecutive bad steps "pd" mode trigger demotion "indefinite". qn_route_promote_k (default 3): consecutive good PD steps "indefinite" mode trigger promotion "pd". Two FD-Hessian refresh triggers rebuild BkB_k fresh finite-difference Hessian iteration stuck "indefinite" mode: qn_fd_refresh_k (default 3): ρk\rho_k qn_route_demote_rho many consecutive steps signals SR1 drift. qn_stuck_refresh_k (default 100): iteration spent many steps "indefinite" mode without promoting, indicating secondary-saddle stall per-step ρ\rho acceptable global progress halted. result list arcopt_qn() now includes qn_fd_refreshes, number FD Hessian refreshes performed optimization. Empirical: 6-parameter growth-mixture-model symmetric saddle, qn_method = "hybrid" now reaches proper local minimum 50/50 (100%) seeds, matching full-Hessian solvers nlminb, trust, arcopt analytic Hessian. Identity-seeded BFGS optim() reaches 29/50 (58%) benchmark.

title: arcopt 0.1.1 (development)
text: Removed limited-memory quasi-Newton methods ("lbfgs", "lsr1", "lhybrid") Woodbury-identity cubic subproblem solver (R/cubic_woodbury.R). variants intended scale larger problems, cubic subproblem solver still O(n^3) eigendecomposition path, limited-memory B_k deliver scalability advantage practice. removal keeps arcopt_qn() focused 2-500 parameter regime documented package philosophy. Valid qn_method values now "bfgs", "sr1", "hybrid". full limited-memory implementation, including Woodbury solver matching tests, preserved scalable-arc branch future work matrix-free / large-scale ARC (see design/scalable-arcs.qmd).
